title: BlackRock's Rosenberg on Fed Rates, IALT ETF Strategy

title: KKR’s Nuttall Says Trading Private Credit Is ‘Likely to Happen’
summary: KKR & Co.’s co-chief executive officer Scott Nuttall said it was likely that the alternative asset manager would eventually start trading private credit, following early efforts by Apollo Global Management Inc. to create a market for less liquid debt.

title: Investors Say It’s Time for Pemex to Tap Global Debt Markets
summary: Investors say the time is right for state-owned Petroleos Mexicanos SA to sell global bonds for the first time in more than three years.

title: Fed's Cook Says She's Ready to Raise Rates If Inflation Lingers
summary: "The risks remain tilted toward higher inflation," Federal Reserve Governor Lisa Cook says during an event at Stanford University. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Dimon Says JPMorgan Could Spend $20 Billion on Deals
summary: Bloomberg Intelligence's Herman Chan joins Dani Burger on "Bloomberg Deals." JPMorgan Chase CEO Jamie Dimon says, “there might be, in the next couple years, a chance to put $10 billion to $20 billion to work buying something,” adding that JPMorgan is “on the lookout” for a deal. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: US Bonds’ Return to Pre-War Calm Fuels Bets It’ll Be Short-Lived
summary: The return of calm to the US bond market following turbulence stirred up by the Iran war is attracting big moves in options, with some traders wagering it won’t last.

title: Stock Rally Falters on Conflicting US-Iran Signals: Markets Wrap
summary: Wall Street traders left stocks wavering amid mixed signals about prospects for a deal to end the war in Iran and revive energy flows through the vital Strait of Hormuz.

title: Gold Declines as Middle East Risks Reinforce Higher Rates Bets
summary: Gold fell for a second day as concerns that continued conflict in the Middle East will prolong inflation and keep borrowing costs elevated outweighed prospects of a US-Iran peace deal.

title: Stocks Rally Pauses as Investors Take Profits, Parse Iran Talks
summary: US stocks ended Wednesday’s session roughly where they started, as investors took profits in tech names but remained optimistic that an end to the war in the Middle East was near, even as President Donald Trump panned earlier reports of a deal.

title: Fuel-Starved Cuba Dealt Blow as Russian Tanker Changes Course
summary: A Russian tanker carrying over 240,000 barrels of diesel has abandoned its voyage to Cuba and is now sailing southeast after weeks of uncertainty surrounding a shipment meant to help relieve the fuel-starved island’s worsening energy crisis.

title: Jamie Dimon 'On The Lookout' For Next Big Deal, Real-Estate Deals | Bloomberg Deals 5/27/2026
summary: A weekly, midday program that delivers high-impact, editorially driven coverage of the most important corporate transactions shaping the global market. Today's guests: Ancora Alternatives President Jim Chadwick, Newmark President of Leasing for North America Liz Hart, Brown Harris Stevens CEO Bess Freedman, PIMCO Real Estate CEO Europe Annette Kroeger, and AMP Founder Anjney Midha. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Base Metals Decline on Mixed Signals of US-Iran Peace Deal
summary: Industrial metals declined as investors remained concerned about global economic growth due to mixed signals about prospects for a US-Iran deal to end the war.

title: Oil Falls as Traders Weigh Signs of Iran Peace Deal Progress
summary: Oil tumbled on optimism that the US and Iran will reach a peace deal, even as President Donald Trump signaled that the warring sides remain far apart in negotiations.

title: Compute Market is Broken: Midha
summary: Anjney Midha, founder of AMP, founding investor of Anthropic, and former general partner at Andreessen Horowitz, joins Dani Burger on "Bloomberg Deals." AMP aims to secure 1.3 GW of compute for the independent ecosystem over the next four years, deploying roughly $40B across equity, debt, and structured capital.  (Source: Bloomberg)

title: H.B. Fuller Has Great Assets For Potential Buyers: Chadwick
summary: Jim Chadwick, president of Ancora Alternatives, joins Dani Burger on "Bloomberg Deals." Activist investor Ancora is pushing US adhesives maker H.B. Fuller to abandon a proposed takeover of UK-based Advanced Medical Solutions Group. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Three Charts Capturing Micron’s Record Rise to $1 Trillion
summary: Micron Technology Inc.’s incredible rally this year has launched the memory-chip firm into an elite group of stocks worth at least $1 trillion, setting at least one record along the way.

title: Warner Bros. Prices $15 Billion Loan Deal Amid High Demand
summary: Warner Bros. Discovery Inc. priced $15 billion of investment-grade loans on Wednesday, as the media company refinances debt ahead of its pending acquisition by Paramount Skydance Corp.

title: Salesforce Earnings Can Put AI Fears to Bed, Give Stock a Lift
summary: While software stocks rebound from the artificial intelligence-driven wipeout earlier this year, Salesforce Inc. hasn’t really benefited. But its earnings after the close Wednesday could pull the company’s shares out of their malaise.

title: The SpaceX IPO Is the Perfect Embodiment of Markets
summary: Momentum trumps everything.

title: PGIM Backs $4B of US Land-Bank Deals in Asset-Based Push
summary: Bloomberg's Scott Carpenter  joins Dani Burger on "Bloomberg Deals." Prudential Financial’s asset-management arm has financed about $4 billion of land-banking projects through a partnership with Domain Real Estate Partners, part of a push to gain exposure to the US homebuilding industry. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Copasa Weighs Relaunch of Offering After Bids Fall Below Minimum Price
summary: Minas Gerais state officials are considering a do-over of Copasa’s privatization process after two bids for a 30% stake in the Brazilian water utility came in below the government’s minimum price, according to people familiar with the matter.

title: Uber Proposes Delivery Hero Takeover at $11.6B Valuation
summary: Bloomberg's Natalie Lung joins Dani Burger on "Bloomberg Deals." Uber has offered to take over Delivery Hero in a deal that would value the German delivery company at about $11.6 billion as it seeks to ramp up competition with DoorDash outside the US.  (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Lilly Goes on $20B Buying Spree as It Seeks Next Act
summary: Bloomberg's Michelle Davis joins Dani Burger on "Bloomberg Deals." Eli Lilly is on a record spending spree, announcing acquisitions worth more than $20 billion so far in 2026 as the drugmaker expands beyond its blockbuster obesity franchise.  (Source: Bloomberg)

title: German Gas Grids Urge Overhaul of Storage Filling Incentives
summary: Germany’s gas grid operators are calling for sweeping changes to the way storage refills are encouraged, warning that stubbornly low inventory levels are heightening the risk of supply shortages ahead of winter.

title: Goldman Says Iran War Dollar Surge Weighed on Treasury Demand
summary: A stronger dollar during the first month of the US-Iran conflict spurred foreign official institutions to sell Treasuries, according to Goldman Sachs.

title: Boeing Rises as CEO Touts 737 Output, New Jet Approvals Nearing
summary:
Boeing Co.’s Chief Executive Officer gave investors an optimistic forecast for this year and beyond, as the US planemaker raises production on its workhorse 737 Max jet, nears certification on long-delayed models and expects a windfall from defense spending. The planemaker has successfully completed a so-called capstone review with the Federal Aviation Administration to further increase production of the 737 Max to 47-jet monthly output, a key step to improve profitability and generate cash, Kelly Ortberg said at the Bernstein Strategic Decisions Conference on Wednesday. 

The company is in the final flight testing stages for the 737 Max 7 and 10 variants, and the intercontinental 777X model, Ortberg said. Still, the certification of seats and the supplies of engines from GE Aerospace for the 787 Dreamliner are bottlenecks that are delaying deliveries.

We get reaction from Sid Philip, Chief Aviation Correspondent for Bloomberg News. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Micron Joins Trillion-Dollar Club | Open Interest 5/27/2026
summary: Get a jump start on the US trading day with Matt Miller and Dani Burger on "Bloomberg Open Interest." AI powers markets to fresh highs, with Goldman calling for even more gains. Micron joins the trillion-dollar club as the chip frenzy accelerates. Nasdaq President Nelson Griggs joins Open Interest on the battle for blockbuster listings and the SpaceX IPO. Plus, Northern Trust’ Asset Management President Michael Hunstad explains why he’s staying risk-on in the AI boom. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: White House Reviews CFTC Plan to Supervise Prediction Markets
summary: The White House is reviewing a new US Commodity Futures Trading Commission proposal that would lay out guidelines for event contracts, the agency’s latest move to mark its turf as it battles with states over who gets to regulate the exploding prediction markets.
